sourceMovie.title: Caught Stealing
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Caught Stealing (2025) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: American Psycho draws from the same crime well as Caught Stealing, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2000 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Batman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Batman (2022) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Caught Stealing later expanded. If Caught Stealing resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: Carlito's Way
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime appeal of Caught Stealing — that dark edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into Carlito's Way (1993). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: A Walk Among the Tombstones
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind A Walk Among the Tombstones (2014) clearly understood what makes crime work — the same quality that keeps Caught Stealing rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Suburbicon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Suburbicon (2017) is the kind of film Caught Stealing fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The crime overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Killer's Kiss
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Killer's Kiss shares Caught Stealing's gift for crime — the 1955 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Blood Ties
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Caught Stealing compelling — the crime, the thriller — shows up in Blood Ties (2013) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Married to the Mob
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime energy of Caught Stealing is alive in Married to the Mob (1988), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Gloria
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Gloria (1980) operates in the same crime as Caught Stealing, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Shade
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Shade (2003) belongs to the same conversation as Caught Stealing — both anchor their crime in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

faq.question: What should I watch after Caught Stealing?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, The Batman, Carlito's Way—each shares DNA with Caught Stealing while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Caught Stealing?
faq.answer: Caught Stealing (2025) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Caught Stealing worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
